scenario_title: add_rr and delete_rrset on the same name/type in one UPDATE
test_rationale: This test isolates the underspecified interaction between an add_rr and a delete_rrset for the same owner/type in a single RFC 2136 UPDATE. The zone starts with y.campus.edu. A absent, so the update is syntactically valid and the only question is whether the server processes the add before the delete, the delete before the add, or rejects the conflict. A follow-up QUERY on y.campus.edu. A makes the resulting state observable, yielding a binary oracle between implementations that preserve the added RRset and those that remove it.

group_1.behavior: Accepts the UPDATE and leaves y.campus.edu. A absent, treating delete_rrset as applying to the message as a whole or to the post-add state.
group_1.post_state: y.campus.edu. has no A RRset (NXDOMAIN or NODATA depending on server/view behavior).

group_2.behavior: Accepts the UPDATE and leaves y.campus.edu. A present, treating delete_rrset as operating only on pre-state or allowing the add to survive.
group_2.post_state: y.campus.edu. A rrset contains y.campus.edu. 500 IN A 192.0.2.22.

expected_divergence.why_divergence: RFC 2136 §3.4.3 defines add_rr and delete_rrset individually, but does not specify the effective ordering when both target the same owner/type in one UPDATE. RFC 2181 §5.1 and §5.4 require RRset coherence but do not resolve whether a just-added RRset can be deleted immediately within the same transaction or whether the delete is evaluated only against pre-update state. Implementations may therefore differ in reply acceptance and final zone contents.
